title: Eric Davis: “A US President Was Briefed on UFOs!”
summary: Eric Davis discusses his involvement in UAP research programs including AASAP and AATIP, his briefings to Congressional committees, the challenges of classified testimony, and his interactions with various government agencies regarding crash retrieval programs. He also addresses breakthrough propulsion physics, the lack of reverse-engineered alien vehicles, and foreign adversary UAP programs.

acronyms.expansion: Advanced Aerospace Weapon System Applications Program
acronyms.definition: A classified DIA program studying UAP phenomena
A task force studying UAP within the Department of Defense
Intelligence agency responsible for satellite reconnaissance

acronyms.expansion: Defense Office of Prepublication and Security Review
acronyms.definition: Office that approves public testimony and publications for security concerns
A type of classified information concerning or derived from sensitive intelligence sources
Highly classified programs with additional access controls beyond normal classification

acronyms.definition: CIA center dealing with weapons proliferation intelligence

acronyms.definition: Organizations that assist government agencies with research and development
Documents used to create waived special access programs under presidential authority
Alleged reverse-engineered craft based on recovered UAP technology

acronyms.definition: Theory describing the strong nuclear force and interactions between quarks and gluons

events.description: CIA shut down UAP crash retrieval programs after decades of no progress
Eric Davis was hired by CIA to be branch chief science scientist of WINPAC
Eric Davis briefed Senate Select Committee on Intelligence staff in classified settings
Eric Davis briefed another group of Congressional staff in Huntsville

key_claims.claim: Dave Grusch was physically harassed at his home and in his vehicles by unknown persons after filing his whistleblower complaint
Someone illegally accessed Dave Grusch's private medical files and leaked his PTSD episode to news media
George H.W. Bush was briefed on a major UAP event from the early 1960s when he was CIA Director, not as President
Bush was denied access to UAP film evidence because he didn't have a need to know since it was DoD intelligence
Harry Reid's request for SAP-level access for AASAP was denied by Deputy Secretary of Defense William Lynn because Reid used political muscle instead of proper security channels
Glen Gaffney blocked an aerospace defense firm from releasing crash retrieval materials to BAASS under DIA contract
There are no alien reproduction vehicles (ARVs) - no one-to-one reproductions or aircraft leveraging recovered UAP technology exist
Russia has at least one crash retrieval, confirmed through classified intelligence from a three-letter agency asset
Dave Grusch found 40 witnesses after being given briefing data by Eric Davis and Hal Puthoff
In 1989, multiple companies were told to return crash retrieval materials when programs were shut down, but at least one company retained materials
Classification can be maintained up to 75 years for certain programs
China has at least one crash retrieval based on information from Hal Puthoff
